rounds.prompt: How many times more visitors does the Louvre get per year than people live in Iceland?
rounds.fact: Iceland has ~370,000 people; the Louvre gets ~9 million visitors per year — 24× more visitors than Iceland has residents.

rounds.prompt: How many times more emails are sent per day than letters were sent per year in 1980?

itemB.name: Emails sent per day today (~350 billion)

rounds.fact: ~300 billion physical letters were sent worldwide in 1980 (per year); today ~350 billion emails are sent every single day — 427× the annual letter count, daily.

rounds.prompt: How many times more data does Netflix stream per day than all internet traffic in 2000?
rounds.fact: In 2000, all internet traffic was ~100 TB/day; Netflix alone streams ~500+ TB/day today — 5× more than the entire 2000 internet.

rounds.prompt: How many times more powerful is a modern MRI scanner than Earth's magnetic field?
rounds.fact: Earth's magnetic field is ~50 microtesla; a typical MRI scanner is 1.5–3 Tesla — about 30,000–60,000× stronger.

rounds.prompt: How many times more books has the Library of Congress than a typical school library?
rounds.fact: A typical school library holds ~10,000 books; the Library of Congress holds ~17 million — 1,700× more, making it the largest library in the world.
